text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: Malaria control efforts currently depend on things like chemically treated bed nets
zh: 目前的疟疾防控措施主要依赖化学处理过的蚊帐等手段，

text: and spraying against mosquitoes,
zh: 以及喷洒杀蚊药物，

text: but scientists keep trying to find other ways to prevent the disease.
zh: 但科学家们仍在努力寻找预防这种疾病的其他方法。

text: A number of vaccines remain under development.
zh: 目前有多种疫苗正在研发中。

text: Most contain genetically engineered versions of a few proteins from the Plasmodium parasite.
zh: 大多数疫苗含有经过基因工程改造的疟原虫蛋白质。

text: Plasmodium is the organism that causes malaria.
zh: 疟原虫是引发疟疾的病原体。

text: These modified proteins are designed to get the body's defenses
zh: 这些改造后的蛋白质旨在激发人体的防御系统，

text: to launch an immune response against the Plasmodium.
zh: 使其对疟原虫产生免疫反应。

text: But the parasite contains thousands of proteins.
zh: 但这种寄生虫体内含有数千种蛋白质。

text: Another experimental vaccine includes a deactivated version of the entire parasite.
zh: 另一种实验性疫苗则使用整个寄生虫的灭活版本。

text: Robert Seder is a researcher at the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases near Washington.
zh: 罗伯特·塞德是华盛顿附近国家过敏症与传染病研究所的研究员。

text: So instead of picking out one or two or three genes,
zh: 与其只挑选一两个或三个基因，

text: you have the potential for what we call breath,
zh: 不如利用我们所说的“广度”潜力，

text: generating an immune response that would be broad rather than narrower.
zh: 产生更广泛而非狭窄的免疫反应。

text: Radiation is used to weaken the parasite so it cannot make people sick
zh: 研究人员用辐射来削弱寄生虫，使其既不会使人患病，

text: or get spread by a mosquito. To make the vaccine,
zh: 也不会通过蚊子传播。为了制成疫苗，

text: scientists use the parasite at a time in its growth when the organism is called a sporozoite.
zh: 科学家们在寄生虫发育的某个阶段——即所谓的“子孢子”阶段——加以利用。

text: The idea has been known since the 1960s.
zh: 这一想法早在上世纪60年代就已提出。

text: But Mr. Sater says a discovery by a researcher at a vaccine company cleared the way for progress.
zh: 但塞德表示，一家疫苗公司研究人员的一项发现为这项研究扫清了障碍。

text: The major breakthrough here was that my collaborator, Stephen Hoffman at Cenaria,
zh: 重大突破在于，我的合作者、Cenaria公司的斯蒂芬·霍夫曼，

text: developed a method where he could isolate the sporozoites and purify them
zh: 开发出一种方法，能够分离并提纯子孢子，

text: so that they could administer it as a vaccine to humans.
zh: 从而使其能够作为疫苗接种到人体中。

text: And no one thought that that was possible. But
zh: 此前没人认为这是可能的。不过

text: no one knew either if the weakened sporozoites would activate the immune system to protect against malaria.
zh: 也没人知道被削弱的子孢子是否能激活免疫系统以抵御疟疾。

text: So researchers tested it on volunteers
zh: 于是研究人员在志愿者身上进行了测试，

text: and found that it was safe, but it was not very effective.
zh: 结果发现疫苗是安全的，但效果并不理想。

text: Only two out of 44 volunteers were protected when bitten by malaria -infected mosquitoes.
zh: 在44名志愿者中，只有2人在被携带疟疾的蚊子叮咬后得到了保护。

text: To find out why, the researchers tested the vaccine on laboratory animals.
zh: 为了找出原因，研究人员在实验动物身上测试了这种疫苗。

text: They decided that the problem was the way the vaccine had been given to the volunteers.
zh: 他们认为问题出在给志愿者接种疫苗的方式上。

text: It was injected into the skin to simulate the bite of a mosquito.
zh: 当时疫苗是注射到皮肤中，以模拟蚊子叮咬的方式。

text: Mr. Sater says it would have been more effective if it had been given directly into the blood.
zh: 塞德表示，如果直接注射到血液中，效果会更好。

text: However, vaccines are generally given by mouth or injected into the skin or muscle.
zh: 然而，疫苗通常是通过口服或注射到皮肤、肌肉中给药的。

text: Having to inject it into the blood could make vaccination programs more difficult
zh: 如果必须注射到血液中，

text: if the vaccine is approved for general use.
zh: 一旦该疫苗获批广泛使用，接种项目可能会更加复杂。

text: For now, more testing is needed.
zh: 目前，还需要进行更多测试。

text: A report on the testing appeared in the journal Science.
zh: 一份关于该测试的报告发表在《科学》杂志上。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂，为您带来美国之音特别英语报道。
